root_motivation: The multidimensional-query-object hypothesis must be a falsifiable number before it can drive product decisions: we need a head-to-head benchmark that proves (or disproves) that the object interface reduces invalid-entity errors and improves accuracy vs raw text-to-SQL, on the same NL-question corpus, with a configurable external grader that prevents the benchmark from grading its own work.
user_persona: AtScale engineer or PM running the benchmark locally or in CI to produce evidence for the 'semantic layer for AI' vision; must work with no live cluster via bundled fixtures.

acceptance_criteria.description: Runs both arms over a tasks file and produces a per-question result table (one row per task, each carrying arm_a and arm_b outputs).
Emits aggregate accuracy, invalid-entity rate, retry count, latency, and token deltas between the two arms; delta fields equal arm_b minus arm_a.
Result equivalence judged by a configurable external grader (shell-out); grader command is not hardcoded.
Report names the winning arm per metric and writes both JSON and markdown formats; every winner field is one of arm_a_sql / arm_b_mqo / tie.
Runs green end-to-end on bundled fixture (recorded arm outputs + stub grader); no live cluster or model key required.
cargo test --release passes; clippy clean (deny warnings).

scope: Arm A: text-to-SQL run_query path (fixture-driven for tests; live path reserved for future work)
Arm B: MQO query_multidimensional path (fixture-driven for tests)
External grader invocation via shell-out; grader receives JSON file {a:[...], b:[...]}
Aggregate report: accuracy, invalid-entity rate, retries, latency, tokens — each with delta and named winner
Per-question result table in JSON and Markdown
Bundled fixture set (3 tasks) + stub grader for offline tests
non_goals: Live cluster integration (no real AtScale MCP calls in this crate; fixture-only for tests)
Implementing or bundling the grader logic (grader is always external shell-out)
Statistical significance testing, confidence intervals, or p-values
Multi-model or multi-grader combinatorial runs in a single invocation
Web UI, TUI, or any interactive output format beyond JSON+Markdown

additional.forbid_unsafe_code: enforced via #![forbid(unsafe_code)] in both lib.rs and main.rs
additional.keystone_deps: mqo-spec (path dep), mqo-mcp-server (transitive); both rebuilt via autobuilder

five_whys_trace.q: Why build a benchmark CLI instead of writing a one-off script?
five_whys_trace.a: The hypothesis ('multidimensional beats tabular for AI analytics') needs to be repeatable across corpus updates and referenced by name in vision documents, not re-derived each time.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does it need to be repeatable and citable?
five_whys_trace.a: The 20%→95% accuracy claim in Self-Assessment Dossier PTC/4713087073 is cited externally; a benchmark without a reproducible binary is not citeable — it's just a number someone typed.

five_whys_trace.q: Why does the benchmark test the interface (SQL vs MQO object) rather than just measuring accuracy on a shared query path?
five_whys_trace.a: The object interface is the unique value proposition of the MQO vision; if a schema-aware SQL prompt matches MQO accuracy, the interface layer adds no value and the vision needs to be revised.

five_whys_trace.q: Why include invalid-entity rate as a first-class metric, not just accuracy?
five_whys_trace.a: Hallucinated measure/dimension names in SQL are a silent failure (wrong rows, no error); the MQO arm fails loudly on invalid entities. If MQO's hallucination rate is lower, that's a stronger safety claim than accuracy alone.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Does 'configurable grader' mean the grader binary must accept exactly one positional argument (the JSON file path)?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Yes. The protocol is: grader_cmd <input_json_file>; grader emits GraderVerdict JSON on stdout. This is the contract verified in AC3.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Should the benchmark call the live MQO/SQL endpoints in tests?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: No. AC5 explicitly requires offline operation via bundled fixtures. Live calls are deferred to a future integration-test crate.

ambiguities_resolved.question: Is mqo-spec a path dependency (local) or a crates.io dependency?
ambiguities_resolved.resolution: Path dependency at ../mqo-spec, rebuilt via autobuilder. No crates.io publication required for tests to pass.
